text:
Market today: Risk-on day led by mega-cap tech - Meta's 10% pop on AI monetization is lifting sentiment across cloud and AI names. That's your world. The Fed noise is fading (Warsh telling traders to watch data, not the Fed) which removes a ceiling for growth stocks near-term.

Your holdings: SPAL (-9.86%) is a leveraged ETF already down hard with a tight stop at $21.38 - one bad session away from getting stopped. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec down big with stop at $9.50 - real stop-out risk, margin amplifies this pain. SOXL (-9.17%) is 3x leveraged chips also bleeding - decay + drawdown is a double tax on margin accounts.

Watch: Whether today's AI/cloud enthusiasm (Meta catalyst) bleeds into chip names like SMH/SOXL - that's your tell for sector rotation. Also watch SPAL and PTNM stops intraday.

Bottom line: Protect the three leveraged/spec losers before anything else - one stop-out on margin hurts more than any new winner helps.
plays: SPAL: Stop is $21.38 - honor it without hesitation. Leveraged ETFs in drawdown on margin are the most dangerous thing in your book right now.
PTNM: Down 15.25% and thinly traded - if it hits $9.50, exit. No reason to let a tiny spec become a margin call trigger.
CRWD: Already up 8.32% and scanner shows Grade A long bias with entry $773.75-$775.10, stop $769.23. No new add needed but trail your stop up if you're comfortable locking gains.
